commit.message:
fix: wrap file upload in try/catch to prevent stuck uploading state

If /api/upload returns non-JSON (e.g. Render 500 HTML error), res.json()
would throw and uploading/uploadingIdx state would stay true forever,
permanently disabling the upload button. Fixed in cart, academy, and admin
store pages using finally block to guarantee state is reset.

commit.message:
Cc reduction tier2 (#297)

* refactor: reduce cognitive complexity across 20+ functions (D+E+F: 22→14)

Systematic CC reduction guided by ControlPlane cross-channel convergence:
- Dispatch table pattern for if/elif chains (6 functions)
- Class extraction for stateful parsers (_MdParser in pages_publisher.py)
- Function hoisting for pure nested functions (properties.py, purity.py)
- Composition decomposition for multi-path functions (composer.py)
- File splits: theory_extractor.py → theory_discovery.py + theory_scoring.py,
  reporter.py → reporter_budget.py + reporter_sections.py

Metrics: avg CC 5.00→4.86, A+B 90.4%→91.2%, worst CC 94→36.
All 5,985 tests pass with 0 regressions. Net -788 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve qlty gate blockers and reduce structural complexity

- Fix ruff SIM103 in context_guidance.py (return condition directly)
- Fix ruff F821 in purity.py (add missing Any import under TYPE_CHECKING)
- Fix ruff SIM102 in source_mapper.py (merge nested if statements)
- Fix type-var issue in cochange_analysis.py (explicit float cast)
- Fix call-overload in structure_patterns.py (type ignore comment)
- Reduce CC in context_bootstrap.py: extract _resolve_model_profile,
  _write_drafts, _extract_dead_paths helpers (CC 27→~12, CC 21→~10)
- Reduce too-many-args in test_channel.py: introduce SymbolGateContext
  dataclass (10 params → 5 + context object)
- Reduce too-many-args in structure_logic.py: introduce
  StructureSnapshotInputs dataclass (10 params → 1 dataclass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Rohan Vinaik <rohanpvinaik@gmail.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tune physics: increase motor/brake torque, reduce gravity

Runner was collapsing into a pile because torque values (0.4) were
way too weak relative to gravity (1.6). The motors couldn't fight
the weight of the torso at all.

Fixes:
- HIP_MAX_TORQUE: 0.4 -> 2.5 (strong enough to swing legs)
- KNEE_MAX_TORQUE: 0.3 -> 2.0
- BRAKE_TORQUE: 0.15 -> 2.0 (stiff joints when idle = runner stands)
- MOTOR_GAIN: 5.0 -> 8.0 (faster motor response)
- HIP/KNEE_MOTOR_SPEED: 0.08 -> 0.12
- Gravity: 1.6 -> 1.2 (still heavy but not crushing)
- Angular damping: 0.95 -> 0.96

The key insight: high brake torque lets the runner stand upright
when no keys are pressed (joints resist collapsing). Pressing keys
overcomes the braking, making the runner unstable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve critical bugs, modernize codebase, fix all lint errors

Core engine fixes:
- Fix WeakRef event listeners silently GC'd (replaced with strong refs)
- Fix scheduler race condition (snapshot batch before execution)
- Fix branded types structurally distinct (ItemId ≠ GroupId)
- Fix double-refilter in ITEMS_LOADED (single setState + refilter)
- Add O(1) filteredIdSet via Set for item visibility checks
- Add registry item cache with invalidation on mutation
- Fix search remove() from O(n) rebuild to O(k) direct deletes
- Fix frecency Temporal.Duration to pre-computed hour constants

React adapter fixes:
- Fix dual React instance (pnpm.overrides forces single canary)
- Add machine disposal on unmount with Strict Mode safety
- Fix controlled dialog render loop via ref tracking
- Add useSyncExternalStore to useCommandState for proper subscriptions
- Fix useOptimistic active item with O(1) filteredIdSet.has()
- Fix async-items infinite re-suspension via stable promise ref
- Add proper mergeRefs utility in primitives
- Change role="application" to role="search" (WAI-ARIA)
- Broaden React peer deps to >=19.0.0

Build & tooling:
- Fix workspace build ordering (--workspace-concurrency=4)
- Disable twoslash (incompatible with TypeScript 6 / @typescript/vfs)
- Optimize lefthook pre-push with glob filter
- Fix all Biome lint errors across 60+ files (noNonNullAssertion,
  useExplicitType, useExhaustiveDependencies, noUnusedImports)

Documentation:
- Add comprehensive READMEs for all 6 packages
- Add LICENSE files for all packages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix flying runner: switch motors from setAngularVelocity to body.torque

Root cause: setAngularVelocity bypasses physics, letting legs spin freely.
Heavy feet (20x density) hitting ground at speed created massive collision
impulses that launched the body into the sky like a catapult.

Fixes:
- Joint motors now use body.torque (like Box2D revolute motors)
  Torque creates angular ACCELERATION that must fight gravity/inertia
- Foot density: 0.02 -> 0.004 (4x limb, was 20x)
- Foot friction: 5.0 -> 3.0
- Motor speeds: 0.15 -> 0.08 rad/frame
- Angular damping: 0.97 -> 0.95 (stronger spin resistance)
- Tighter joint angle limits
- Barrel roll easter egg also converted to torque-based

The runner should now fall when buttons are held wrong, not fly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix QR preview disappearing and improve preview sizing

Root cause: OptionsPanel and PreviewPanel were defined inside App,
so React remounted them (and destroyed previewRef's DOM node) on every
state change.

Fixes:
- Move OptionsPanel outside App as a stable component
- Inline the preview box directly in App's JSX (no sub-component)
- Switch to SVG type so preview scales naturally with CSS max-width
- Remove complex transform-scale approach, preview fills container cleanly

commit.message:
fix: _cmd_prefix() always uses cmd.exe /c on Windows (no conditional)

The previous fix still had conditional .cmd/.bat extension checks that
fell through to bare "claude" when shutil.which returned None or a
non-.cmd path. On Windows, cmd.exe /c handles ALL cases: .cmd wrappers,
.bat files, bare names (resolved via PATHEXT), and .exe binaries.

feat: 스킬 개발 도구의 Git Hygiene 개선

.skill 패키지에 캐시 파일(.DS_Store, __pycache__, .skill-lifecycle/)가
포함되는 문제를 해결하고, 다른 사용자 환경에서도 안정적으로
동작하도록 개선합니다.

변경 사항:
- skill-creator/init_skill.py: .gitignore 자동 생성 기능 추가
- skill-creator.md: Git Hygiene 가이드 섹션 추가
- skill-lifecycle: .gitignore 추가, 패키지 크기 175KB → 26KB 최적화
- skill-eval: .gitignore 추가, Git hygiene 검증 항목 추가
- skills/.gitignore: 전역 .gitignore 템플릿 추가

주요 개선:
- 캐시 파일이 Git에 커밋되는 것을 방지
- 개인 경로 하드코딩 제거 (환경 변수 사용)
- OMC 상태 파일(.omc/, .notepad.md) 제외
- 새 스킬 생성 시 .gitignore 자동 포함

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): fix YAML block scalar indentation in workflow heredocs

Heredoc content at column 0 inside run: | blocks terminates the YAML
block scalar in strict parsers (used by GitHub for workflow name
registration). This prevented GitHub from extracting the name: field,
causing file paths to appear as workflow names.

Replaced cat <<EOF heredoc patterns with properly indented echo/printf
constructs that keep all content within the YAML block scalar's
indentation level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add tactical cheat sheet, detachment picker, fix crusade rules data

- Fix build-data.ts to include crusade_rules and boarding_actions from
  source JSON (was silently dropping them). Rebuilt all data — 22 factions
  now have crusade rules populated.
- Add detachment_id to CampaignPlayer type and setDetachment to context.
- Add detachment dropdown picker on Roster page.
- Add phase parser utility (phaseParser.ts) that maps stratagems and
  abilities to game phases (command/movement/shooting/charge/fight) with
  timing (your turn/opponent/either).
- Add Tactical Cheat Sheet page (/cheat-sheet) with phase tab bar,
  expandable stratagem/ability cards, source unit badges, and
  non-prescriptive language.
- Fix bug: campaign archive stored faction_id as faction_name.
- Fix bug: leaveCampaign cleared storage after state updates (race).
- Fix bug: FactionPicker z-index conflicted with BottomNav.

commit.message:
Patch5: multi-section regression fix, bidirectional qualifier propagation, V11 wide-table warning

- Fix table_38-style regression: multi-section instruction now prefers
  section with most data rows (primary analytical table) instead of
  forcing first section. Group-header detection scoped to within the
  extracted section only. Recovers 5-col regression output.
- Bidirectional nearest qualifier propagation: heuristic fallback uses
  year-distance to choose between left/right qualified neighbors
  (LEFT wins ties), replacing always-left strategy.
- V11 warning: non-blocking flag when >12 bare year columns with
  header_row_count<=1, flagging possible missed qualifier row
  (table_14-style long-table limitation).
- Prompt hint for wide tables (15+ year columns) to check for
  second qualifier row.

Targeted: 5/5 PASS (table_38 fixed, table_7/14 stable, guards OK)
Sample30: 30/30 PASS, 0 fallbacks (-2 vs baseline), -33% wall clock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add CLAUDE.md with project architecture and conventions (#3)

* Add CLAUDE.md with project architecture and conventions

Documents the full service architecture, tech stack, environment
variables, routing, and a checklist for adding new services (used
as a guide for the upcoming MailHog integration).

https://claude.ai/code/session_016K5vPJPyrWPMN7zMUjZU7j

* Add Mailpit email testing integration

- docker-compose.yml: add axllent/mailpit service with SMTP (1025),
  web UI (8025), and Traefik labels routing mailpit.localcloudkit.com:3030
- setup-mkcert.sh: extend cert SAN to include mailpit.localcloudkit.com
- setup-hosts.sh: add mailpit.localcloudkit.com to /etc/hosts setup
- server.js: add GET /mailpit/stats and DELETE /mailpit/messages endpoints
  proxying to the Mailpit internal API
- api.ts: add mailpitApi.stats() and mailpitApi.clearMessages()
- MailpitBadge.tsx: new header badge polling stats every 30s, showing
  unread count (red) or total (blue), with green/grey health dot;
  clicking opens mailpit web UI in a new tab
- Dashboard.tsx: mount MailpitBadge in the header nav
- env.example: add MAILPIT_INTERNAL_URL and MAILPIT_SMTP_PORT
- Makefile: add mailpit-logs and mailpit-clear targets
- docs/MAILPIT.md: usage guide with SDK examples and API reference

https://claude.ai/code/session_016K5vPJPyrWPMN7zMUjZU7j

* Add existing users setup note to MAILPIT.md

Clearly documents the two one-time steps needed for users who already
have LocalCloud Kit running: re-run setup-mkcert.sh (cert SAN update)
and setup-hosts.sh (new hosts entry), then make restart.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016K5vPJPyrWPMN7zMUjZU7j

* feat(mailpit): add CLI test email docs and unify service polling

- Add Sending a Test Email section to MAILPIT.md with swaks, curl (macOS/Linux), curl (Windows CMD), and Python options
- Add Polling for Live Counts subsection under API
- Introduce useServicesData hook replacing useLocalStackData — single 5s poll returning separate localstack, mailpit, and redis objects
- Add MailpitStats and RedisStatus types to types/index.ts
- Refactor MailpitBadge to accept stats as props, removing its independent 30s polling loop
- Update mailpitApi.stats() return type to MailpitStats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  (including nested Map and List children) before sending to DynamoDB

* docs(changelog): update unreleased with dynamodb number validation fix

* fix(gui): resolve sub-modal click bubbling, log scroll, and nav restructure

- BucketViewer: move FileViewerModal and UploadFileModal outside backdrop
  div to fix click-to-close interfering with S3 file uploads
- BucketViewer: add missing closing div for flex-1 content wrapper

* docs(changelog): update unreleased with bug fixes and nav restructure

* docs(readme): add Mailpit and Redis badges, update features and service URLs

- Updated tagline to "Local Cloud Development Environment"

- Added Mailpit Email Testing feature section under features
- Added Mailpit UI and SMTP rows to the Service URLs table
- Added Mailpit access URLs to the Quick Start section
- Linked docs/MAILPIT.md under Service Documentation
- Updated Latest Release blurb to reflect v0.7.1 changes
- Updated CHANGELOG with docs entry

* docs(connect): move to docs/, add Secrets Manager examples, fix Go SDK

- Relocated CONNECT.md → docs/CONNECT.md alongside other service docs
- Added Secrets Manager SDK examples for JavaScript, Python, Go, and Java
  covering list, create, get, and delete operations
- Fixed deprecated Go EndpointResolverWithOptionsFunc; now uses per-service

- Added credentials import and UsePathStyle for Go S3 client
- Added troubleshooting table and per-language debug logging snippets
- Updated README.md and GETTING_STARTED.md links to docs/CONNECT.md

No AWS account needed, no cost, full visibility. localcloud-kit is a plug-and-play development toolkit built on LocalStack that lets you build, test, and explore cloud resources like S3, DynamoDB, and more. All from your local machine. Comes with a built-in UI for inspecting data and debugging

조건부 렌더링으로 변경하여 모달이 열릴 때마다 리마운트되도록 수정.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: relax consensus guard in paper mode, enforce forced exploration

Paper/bootstrap mode needs trade data to learn. The consensus guard
was blocking ALL trades when <3 of 8 domains had signals (common in
strong trends). Now advisory-only in paper mode. Forced exploration
(>6h no trades) is now highest-priority override that cannot be
blocked by consensus guard, regime win rate, or calibration warnings.

commit.message:
fix: resolve 5 client-reported bugs and add API performance monitoring

Bug fixes:
- BUG-001: iPhone geolocation — add enableHighAccuracy, timeout, maximumAge to all getCurrentPosition calls
- BUG-002: Android map pick — sync marker with center prop, add RecenterMap component, import useMap
- BUG-003: Ticket dept filter — add useEffect for selectedDept, pass dept explicitly to avoid stale closure
- BUG-004: Payment "No data" — persist context to sessionStorage, handle both API response formats
- BUG-005: Camera access error — remove premature permissions.query check, add stopScanning on retry, re-throw permission errors instead of blind fallback

API monitoring:
- Add centralized apiPerfMonitor across all 6 API service files
- Replace console.log/error with structured logger in fofiApis, orderApis, registrationApis, customer/apis
- Auto-report on visibility change and dev interval in main.jsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(render): add span-aware tile diff builder; fix(runtime): guard reconcile_subscriptions behind self.running

## ftui-render: span-aware tile diff building

Add `build_from_buffer()` method to `TileDiffBuilder` that accepts a
`&Buffer` directly instead of the decomposed `TileDiffInput` struct.
This unlocks access to `Buffer::dirty_span_row()`, which provides
per-row dirty spans indicating which column ranges were actually
modified. The old `build()` method scanned every cell in a dirty row's
dirty_bits slice to accumulate tile counts; the new method narrows the
scan to only the column ranges covered by recorded dirty spans.

Key details:

- Extract `accumulate_tile_counts_range()` as a standalone helper that
  processes a single `Range<usize>` within a row's dirty_bits slice,
  mapping dirty cells to the correct tile columns and accumulating
  counts. Returns `Err(())` on u32 overflow for early bailout.

- `build_from_buffer()` iterates rows, queries `dirty_span_row(y)`,
  and dispatches: if spans are available and not marked full, iterate
  only the recorded spans; otherwise fall back to the full row range
  (0..width). This means screens with sparse mutations (e.g., a single
  cursor blink or spinner update) skip the vast majority of cells
  during SAT construction.

- The `scanned_cells` counter tracks exactly how many cells were
  visited, surfaced via `stats.sat_build_cells` for observability.

- `compute_dirty_changes()` call site updated to use
  `build_from_buffer(tile_config, new)` instead of manually
  destructuring the buffer into a `TileDiffInput`. This is a pure
  refactor of the call site with identical semantics.

- Add assertion to existing tile-diff-with-spans test verifying that
  `sat_build_cells == 3` (only the 3 explicitly dirtied cells are
  scanned, not the full row widths).

## ftui-runtime: guard reconcile_subscriptions against post-quit calls

Add `&& self.running` guard to two `reconcile_subscriptions()` call
sites in `program.rs`:

1. After screen-dispatch tick processing (~line 3864): previously, if
   the model's update handler issued a quit command during a screen
   tick, the program would still attempt to reconcile subscriptions on
   a model that may have already begun teardown.

2. After force-tick on screen transition (~line 4434): same scenario
   where a force-tick of the newly active screen could trigger a quit,
   and the subsequent reconcile would operate on stale/torn-down state.

Both guards mirror the existing pattern established in commit 6948470
("guard against post-quit subscription reconciliation") and prevent
potential panics or logic errors from reconciling subscriptions after
the program has already decided to stop running.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Kafka job status tracking with SSE for POST /geo/save

Replace fire-and-forget Kafka pattern with tracked async processing.
The save endpoint now creates a PENDING JobStatus in MongoDB, wraps
data in KafkaMessage(trackingId + payload), and returns 202 with
trackingId. Kafka consumer updates job status to COMPLETED/DUPLICATE/
FAILED and pushes real-time updates via SseEmitter to connected clients.

New endpoints:
- GET /geo/status/stream/{trackingId} (SSE)
- GET /geo/status/{trackingId} (REST fallback)
- POST /auth/geo/save (auth proxy with ownerEmail injection)
- GET /auth/geo/status/stream/{trackingId} (SSE proxy)
- GET /auth/geo/status/{trackingId} (REST proxy)
- DELETE /auth/geo/{youtubeContentId} (auth proxy)

Geo content endpoints moved from AuthController to GeoDataController.
Old /auth/adventuretubedata endpoints removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

remove raw event archive step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add interactive setup wizard for first-time configuration

When no valid bot config is found (placeholder values or missing file),
the server launches an interactive wizard that guides the user to fill in
bot_id, secret, relay_url, and name. Config is written to config/bots.yaml
and the server proceeds to start.

Non-interactive environments (Docker, piped stdin) gracefully skip the
wizard and exit with an error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: centralize icon registry for NavIcon and QuickAccess (#598)

Create iconRegistry.tsx as single source of truth for all feature icons.
NavIcon now delegates to the registry instead of a switch statement.
QuickAccessTiles uses iconKey references instead of inline SVGs.

This fixes the child-dev icon mismatch (shield vs crawling toddler)
and ensures all icon updates propagate to nav, quick access, and widgets.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Extract menu descriptions for 10x better allergen detection

Change scan-photo prompt to extract ingredient lists and descriptions
alongside dish names (format: "Name | ingredients, sauces, prep notes").
The engine now sees "Truffle Pasta — house-made fettuccine, black truffle
butter, parmesan, pancetta" instead of just "Truffle Pasta", catching
far more allergens from menu descriptions.

Also bumps max_tokens from 1024 → 4096 to prevent silent truncation
of long menus.

Fix mobile masthead: compact header so page titles aren't hidden

On mobile (≤760px), the fixed masthead was ~300px tall (stacked logo,
nav, locale, live-model bar), burying hero titles behind it. Now:
- Hide secondary row (LIVE PUBLIC MODEL) on mobile
- Shrink logo, hide brand subtitle, reduce gaps/padding
- Place nav on row 2 spanning full width, locale beside brand
- Increase hero padding-top to 8.5rem to clear compact header
- Hide redundant Ideas-page topbar (masthead already has nav)
